series_description:
Territorial limit agreements between
manufacturers and distributors filed with
TABC.

Includes local option election results;
historic wet/dry county information

Field activity plans; operation plans; joint
operation coversheets.

series_title:
Investigation Records,
Enforcement - Not
Sustained/Referred
Investigation Records,
Enforcement - Sustained
Investigation Records,
Enforcement - Sustained,
Felony
series_description:
Allegations not sustained, inconclusive, or
case referred to another agency.
Allegations sustained. Violation is not a
felony.
Allegations sustained. Violation is a felony.

Inspections of regulated entities by TABC
that are not directly tied to an
investigation. Includes inspections
conducted as part of the licensing process.
comments:
CAUTION: If an inspection becomes the
subject of an administrative action,
litigation, or Enforcement or
Compliance investigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of RSIN
1.1.048, RSIN 1.1.073, AIN
ADM1005a/b, or AIN ADM1006a/b/c.

series_description:
Offense reports, disposition reports,
photographs, audiovisual recordings, arrest
reports and other records relating to the
investigation of an alleged offense
committed by a juvenile.
comments:
CAUTION: If a juvenile record becomes
the subject of an administrative action,
litigation, or Enforcement or
Compliance investigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of RSIN
1.1.048, RSIN 1.1.073, AIN
ADM1005a/b, or AIN ADM1006a/b/c.

series_title:
Licensing, Complaint,
Inspection, and
Investigation Basic
Information - Raw Data
series_description:
Basic information pertaining to Licensing
records and Enforcement and Compliance
complaints, inspections, and investigations.
comments:
See AIN ADM1011 for reports produced
from raw data.

series_title:
Licensing, Complaint,
Inspection, and
Investigation Basic
Information - Reports
series_description:
Ad hoc reports on basic information
pertaining to Licensing records and
Enforcement and Compliance complaints,
inspections, and investigations. Includes
reports distributed either internally or to
other entities.
comments:
See AIN ADM1010 for raw data used to
produce reports.

series_description: Industry promotion approval requests.

Periodic forms and reports relating to the
manufacturing, importation, distribution,
shipping, storage, or sale of alcoholic
beverages required by TABC to be regularly
filed by a permittee or licensee.

Includes all records collected and created
by TABC as a part of the label
approval/product registration process,
including application, forms, photographs,
laboratory reports.
comments:
CAUTION: If an application becomes the
subject of an administrative action or
litigation, it must be included in and is
subject to the minimum retention
period of RSIN 1.1.048 or 1.1.073.

series_description: Certificate of Label Approvals (COLAs)

Includes all records associated with
Seller/Server Certification and Texas
Responsible Alcohol Delivery (TRAD)
Certification

Includes all records associated with
seller/server certification training schools
and the Texas Responsible Alcohol Delivery
(TRAD) course providers.

Audits and reviews performed by or on
behalf of TABC, including the working
papers that support the audit. Includes
audits performed by TABC on regulated
entities or audits performed on TABC.
comments:
CAUTION: If an audit becomes the
subject of an administrative action or
litigation, it must be included in and is
subject to the minimum retention
period of RSIN 1.1.048 or 1.1.073.
The State Auditor's Office (SAO) retains
any copies of its audits performed on
Texas state agencies.
See RSIN 5.4.018 and 5.4.019 for Audit
Plan records.

series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests by an
agency.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
Legislative Appropriation Requests must
be submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program per 13
TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A). Working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Complaints and other unsolicited feedback
received from an outside source by TABC
concerning TABC and/or its regulated
entities, and records pertaining to the
resolution of said complaints.
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes the
subject of an administrative action,
litigation, or Enforcement, Compliance,
or Internal Affairs investigation, it must
be included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of RSIN
1.1.048, RSIN 1.1.073, AIN
ADM1005a/b, AIN ADM1006a/b/c, AIN
EMP3026a/b/c/d/e, or AIN EMP3027.
See AIN ADM1010 or ADM1011 for
basic information pertaining to
complaints.

series_title:
Correspondence -
Administrative
(field 71
igger for specific records ser
series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal leadership,
supervisory, managerial, executive,
administrative, substantive, and other high-
level correspondence in any media,
including electronic communication,
pertaining to the formulation, planning,
implementation, interpretation,
modification, or redefinition of the
programs, services, or projects of an
agency and the administrative regulations,
policies, and procedures that govern them.
FE- Fi.seal year end
ies definition LA - life of Asset
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or commission
members, division directors and
program heads require archival review.
Contact the State Archives when these
records have met their retention
periods.
CAUTION: This records series and RSIN
1.1.008 should be used only for
correspondence that is not included in
or directly related to another records
series on the agency's approved
schedule. For example, a memorandum
that documents an appropriations
request must be retained for the
minimum retention period prescribed
by RSIN 1.1.004; a letter concerning an
audit for that prescribed by RSIN
1.1.002, etc. See also RSIN 1.1.011.

ansfer to State/University A re h ivist

series_description:
Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and
internal correspondence, in any media,
including electronic communication,
pertaining to or arising from the routine
operations of the policies, programs,
services, or projects of an agency.
comments:
See comment to RSIN 1.1.007. See also
RSIN 1.1.010.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.

Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency.

Calendars, appointment books or programs
and scheduling, or itinerary records
maintained by staff during business hours
that document appointments, itineraries,
and other activities of agency officials or
employees.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the calendars,
appointment, and itinerary records of
elected officials, executive staff, board
or commission members, division
directors, and program heads require
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have met
their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee to
document his or her work activities may
be a state record and subject to this
retention period. See Open Records
Decision 635 issued in December 1995
by the Attorney General.

series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Attorney
General, including any requests eliciting
the opinions.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include legal
opinions or advice rendered on a
matter in litigation or with regard to
pending litigation, or opinions rendered
for Public Information Act requests. See
RSIN 1.1.048, 1.1.020, and 1.1.021.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Opinions and advice
that set legal precedent or exhibit
historical value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission for archival
preservation.

series_description:
News, press releases, marketing files and
collateral, or any public relations files
maintained or issued by an agency.
Includes print, electronic, audio, and
audiovisual records.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code). Also includes
withdrawn requests.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are excepted under the Public
Information Act (Chapter 552, Government
Code), including records provided to the
legislature under section 552.008.

Charts or diagrams that show the structure
of an agency and the relationships and
relative ranks of its departments and job
positions.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts showing
the overall arrangement and
administrative structure of the state
agency need to be transferred to the
Archives and Information Services
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission. Organizational
charts showing division/department
level detail are not considered archival.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process of
planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
neither included in nor directly related to
other records series in this schedule.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.

Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.

Surveys soliciting feedback from customers
or clients of an agency, and the statistical
data maintained rating an agency's
performance.
comments:
See RSIN 1.1.067 for summary reports
compiled from customer surveys.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches, papers,
and presentations given by or on behalf
of executive staff, board or commission
members, division directors and
program heads require archival review.

series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
developed by an agency associated with
training the entities or non-personnel
individuals it regulates or serves, including
but not limited to training manuals, course
registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets,
syllabuses, course outlines, and similar
training aids used in external training
programs.
comments:
See RSIN 3.3.030 for internal personnel
training materials.
CAUTION: Does not include hazardous
material training records. See RSIN
5.4.007.

series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
developed by TABC associated with
Seller/Server, Texas Responsible Alcohol
Delivery (TRAD), and Retailer Education
and Awareness Program (REAP) training.
comments:
See RSIN 3.3.030 for internal personnel
training materials.

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set legal
precedent or exhibit historical value will
be evaluated by the Archives and
Information Services Division of the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission for archival preservation.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies as
required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed with
the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).

Operational strategic plans, which include
descriptions of information resources
programs, prepared in accordance with
Government Code, 2054.095 and
2056.002. Includes working files and
related documentation used in creating the
final plan.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
Strategic Plans must be submitted to
the Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Self-evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans with Disabilities Act.

Records of temporary usefulness that are
not an integral part of a records series of
an agency, that are not regularly filed
within an agency’s recordkeeping system,
and that are required only for a limited
period of time for the completion of an
action by an official or employee of the
agency or in the preparation of an ongoing
records series. Transitory records are not
essential to the fulfillment of statutory
obligations or to the documentation of
agency functions.
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should make certain records are
not part of another records series listed
in this schedule or, for records series
unique to an agency, are not part of a
records series that documents the
fulfillment of the statutory obligations
of the agency or the documentation of
its functions.
The disposal of transitory information
need not be documented through
destruction signoffs (RSIN 1.2.001) or in
records disposition logs (RSIN 1.2.010),
but agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of these
records as part of its records
management plan (RSIN 1.2.014).

series_title:
Meetings, Agendas and
Minutes of Open
series_description:
Agendas and minutes of state boards,
committees, commissions, and councils
that conduct open meetings as required by
Chapter 551, Government Code.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The archival
requirement will be met by sending a
copy to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State Library
and Archives Commission.

series_title:
Meetings, Agendas and
Minutes or Audiovisual
Recordings of Closed
series_description:
Agendas, minutes, or audiovisual
recordings of closed meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils.
comments: See caution comment at RSIN 1.1.058.

series_description:
Audiovisual recordings of open meetings of
state boards, commissions, committees,
and councils.
comments:
CAUTION: It is an exception to the
AC+90 days retention that if an agency
does not prepare written minutes, it
must retain recordings of open
meetings permanently, following the
same retention requirements of RSIN
1.1.058. See also caution comment at
RSIN 1.1.058.

series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils from which written minutes are
prepared.
comments: See caution comment at RSIN 1.1.058.

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils, including exhibit items,
documentation for agenda items, public
comment forms, etc. Includes documents
sent in advance of meetings for briefing
purposes, some of which may not be
submitted at an actual meeting.
comments: See caution comment at RSIN 1.1.058.

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency
staff meetings.

Any records of an agency needed for the
documentation of output, outcome,
efficiency, and explanatory measures in an
agency's appropriations request or
strategic plan, and for performance
measures used to manage the agency.
comments:
See RSIN 1.1.068 for reports on agency
performance measures.
CAUTION: The FE+3 retention period
overrides any shorter retention period
for a records series in this schedule if
the records series is needed for
documentation of agency performance
measures.

series_title:
Reports and Studies (Non-
Fiscal) – Raw Data
series_description:
Information or data collected and compiled
for the purpose of producing non-fiscal
reports.
comments:
See RSIN 1.1.067 for reports produced
from raw data.
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for information or
data included in or directly related to
another records series in this schedule.
See RSIN 1.1.064.

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating final
report.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program per 13
TAC 3.3. The requirement is met by
sending the required copies of the
reports to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State Library
and Archives Commission. Working files
and related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or
studies on non-fiscal aspects of an agency's
programs, services, or projects compiled by
agency personnel, by advisory committees,
or by consultants under contract with an
agency that are not noted elsewhere in this
schedule. Includes reports distributed
either internally or to other entities.
comments:
See RSIN 1.1.065 for raw data used to
produce reports.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Reports are archival
when they deal with significant aspects
of the agency's programs.

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to
executive and legislative offices.
comments:
See RSIN 1.1.064 for documentation
used to produce reports on agency
performance measures.

series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to
workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, and
similar activities.
comments:
CAUTION: If reports are used to
document performance measures, see
RSIN 1.1.064.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or projects.
Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating rules,
policies, and procedures.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final rules, policies, and procedures,
are not subject to archival requirement
and may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of Attorney
General (OAG) on an agency's Public
Information Act activities, per Government
Code, 552.010.

Transcripts and final decisions of hearings
conducted as part of the regulatory
process, and hearings on proposed rules
and changes.
comments:
CAUTION: These records may be
maintained with related information,
including meeting notices, proofs of
publication, and meeting minutes, and
require a longer retention period.

series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self-study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.

Final agreement described by Government
Code, 2009.054(c), associated with a
matter conducted under an alternative
dispute resolution procedure in which
personnel of a state agency participated as
a party on the agency's behalf.

Subpoenas for production of evidence
produced for litigation in which the state
agency is not a party. Includes legal
documents requiring recipient to appear in
court to testify, or to produce records to
be used in litigation.
comments:
For subpoenas related to litigation in
which the state agency is a party, see
RSIN 1.1.048.

series_description:
Records that document the release of
records or information through any
method other than a Public Information
Act (Chapter 552, Government Code)
request or subpoena (including
employment verification).
comments:
See RSIN 1.1.020 for records released
under the Public Information Act. See
RSIN 1.1.076 for records produced for a
subpoena. See RSIN 1.1.048 for records
produced for litigation.
CAUTION: Some records releases may
require longer retention period.
Agencies must determine if longer
retention period is required based on
any federal or state statutes or
regulations that apply to the agency's
functions. Agency legal staff should be
consulted.

series_description:
Waivers of liability, including statements
signed by volunteers acknowledging non-
entitlement to benefits, agreeing to abide
by state agency policies, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: If an accident occurs to any
person covered by a signed waiver of
liability, it must be retained for the
same period as accident reports. See
RSIN 5.4.001 and 5.4.014a/b.

series_description:
Documents authorizing final disposition of
records under a certified records retention
schedule. Records may also include
destruction authorizations (e.g. form RMD
102) approved by Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
comments:
CAUTION: If destruction authorizations
are maintained as part of RSIN 1.2.010
(Records Disposition Logs), then longer
retention period applies.

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used internally
and externally by the agency, including
subsequent revisions to an agency form or
any associated design or design
modification requests.

A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR
105) that identifies the records that are
created and maintained by an agency,
provides the minimum timeframes the
records must be retained, and includes
instructions for their disposition. This
series may include working files and
documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by the
State and Local Records Management
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of
records to/from onsite and offsite storage
areas or a transfer of physical custody.

Logs or similar records listing records
disposed of by an agency, which might
include records series title, dates of
records, and date of disposition.
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to
capture records inventory information,
including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of a
records series.

Documentation used to provide and
improve access to records. Includes
indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers,
guides, and other finding aids.
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry the
same retention period and archival
code of the records they support.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish the policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.

One copy of each state publication, except
a publication that is subject to a different
retention period in this schedule. A state
publication is information in any format
that is publicly distributed and produced by
the authority of or at the total or partial
expense of a state agency or is required to
be distributed under law by the agency.
The term does not include information the
distribution of which is solely limited to
contractors with or grantees of the agency,
staff persons within the agency or within
other government agencies, or members
of the public under a request made under
the Public Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code).
FE- Fi.seal year end
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications must
be submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program, Texas
State Library and Archives Commission,
by law (Government Code, 441.103-
441.105). The Texas State Library and
Archives Commission will retain a copy
of all state publications submitted to it
on a continuing basis, subject to
periodic evaluation to determine if the
publication merits further retention.
For additional information and
requirements concerning state
publications made available to the
public through the internet, consult the
administrative rules of the Texas State
Publications Depository Program of the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission (13 TAC 3.1-3.8).
l Codes (i:ield 101

series_description:
Physical and digital development and
design files used to create State
Publications, including but not limited to
background material, copy (drafts), original
artwork, photo negatives, prints, flats, etc.
This includes all work performed both
inside and outside the agency.
comments:
See RSIN 1.3.001 for final State
Publications created from development
files.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Successive and
substantive drafts of major publications
may be archival. Major is defined by
both the publication's authorship and
its impact on Texas and Texans. Original
artwork, including photo prints and
negatives that have significant value as
evidence of agency programs as well as
the potential for re-use, may be
archival.

series_description:
Records obtained from external, non-TABC
government databases for
licensing/permitting, seller/server, or
investigatve purposes.

Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets of
complete and accurate electronic records
and associated processing files. Examples
include, but are not limited to, data tables
of relational databases used by
applications or computer programs.
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of AC to
agency master files and application data
that the files do not fall under records
series listed elsewhere.

series_description:
Agency-developed automated software
applications, operating system files, and
associated processing files, including job
control language, programs, applications,
scripts, source code, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for access
to electronic records must be retained
for the period of time required to
access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, equipment
control systems, and associated processing
files.
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for access
to electronic records must be retained
for the period of time required to
access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records; to document
modifications to computer programs; to
access, retrieve, manipulate, and interpret
data in an automated system; and allow
for their timely, authorized disposition.
Records include but are not limited to: user
guides, system or sub-system definitions,
system specifications, input and output
specifications, system flow charts, program
flow charts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, data element dictionaries,
file layouts, code books or tables, and
other records that explain the meaning,
purpose, structure, logical relationships,
and origin of the data elements.
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for access
to electronic records must be retained
for the period of time required to
access the records.

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits.
Records include but are not limited to:
reports showing transactions accepted,
rejected, suspended, and/or processed;
history files or tapes; records of updates to
application files; and security logs.

Hardware and software components for
monitoring agency’s computer system
resources and performance.
comments:
The disposal of monitoring records that
are automatically overwritten need not
be documented through destruction
authorizations (RSIN 1.2.001) or in
records disposition logs (RSIN 1.2.010),
but agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of these
records as part of its records
management plan (RSIN 1.2.014).

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents
establishing data processing policies and
procedures in an agency in such areas as
access and security, systems development,
data retention and disposition, data
ownership, production control, system
back-up, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include technical
documentation of procedures
necessary for reading or processing of
electronic records. See RSIN 2.1.009.

series_description:
Forms and logs used to enter and reconcile
data sets submitted for processing.

A record of web pages visited during an
internet session, including data files of user-
specific information created by the
webserver, that allows users to access
previously visited pages more quickly or to
generate a record of usage of a state-
owned computer.
comments:
The disposal of internet history records
need not be documented through
destruction authorizations (RSIN
1.2.001) or in records disposition logs
(RSIN 1.2.010), but agencies should
establish procedures governing disposal
of these records as part of its records
management plan (RSIN 1.2.014).

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties, and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to
activate the software for legal use by the
end users of a state agency, along with
warranties providing that the software will
perform in accordance with functional
specifications.

Records documenting the request for and
response to help desk tickets received by
divisions or units, such as information
technology.

Biennial information security plan for
protecting the security of the agency’s
information.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents whose
submission by candidates for vacant
positions is required on the application
form, by application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include ADA
Accommodation Requests. See RSIN
3.1.042.

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents whose
submission by candidates for vacant
positions is required on the application
form, by application procedures, or in the
employment advertisement.

Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work-
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisorial level except in those agencies
with counseling staff.

Agency copies of information relating to
the selection by employees of life,
disability, health, vision, dental, disability,
and other types of insurance or benefits
offered by the State of Texas to its
employees.
legal_citation:
CAUTION:
Documents that
serve as payroll
deduction
authorizations must
be maintained for the
retention period
prescribed for RSIN
3.2.001.

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

Includes a contract or agreement regarding
the terms and conditions of employment
of an individual and a contract with a
vendor for temporary staffing services.

comments:
CAUTION: Does not include criminal
history checks; see RSIN 3.1.026.
Does not include drug screening test
results; see RSIN 3.1.040a/b/c.
Does not include pre-employments
skills tests; see RSIN 3.3.027 and
3.3.028.

series_description:
Records relating to the review of employee
grievances or complaints against personnel
policies, working conditions, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency employee
with the Equal Employment Office
(EEO) of the U. S. Department of Labor
(DOL). See RSIN 1.1.048.
Does not include Internal Affairs
investigations. See AIN
EMP3026a/b/c/d/e or EMP3027.

series_description:
Job evaluations, performance appraisals, or
other similar documents used to evaluate
the performance of an employee.

Corrective actions are those actions which
do not affect pay, status, or tenure and are
imposed to correct or improve an
employee's job performance.
comments:
CAUTION: If, during the retention
period of this records series, any part of
this series is used to document and
support personnel disciplinary action
under RSIN 3.1.021, all documentation
from this series used to support
disciplinary action must be retained for
the minimum retention period
described by RSIN 3.1.021.

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions which
may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are
imposed to discipline an employee whose
conduct is harmful to the best interests of
the state, the agency, or the employee
work force; and for failure to improve
performance or conduct following
imposition of corrective action. May
include cause for demotion, suspension, or
dismissal, and the reasons for failure to
give written notice of resignation. May also
be used to document evidence of
employee self-improvement efforts, as
well as favorable and unfavorable
communication.

Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the records
of an employee, including pay grade,
position classification, employee number,
evaluation date, and termination of
employment.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include overtime
personnel action forms. See RSIN
3.4.004.

series_description:
Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.

Medical or physical examination reports or
certificates of employees for whom
periodic monitoring of health or fitness is
required.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include pre-
employment physical examinations. See
RSIN 3.1.014.

series_description:
Pre-employment background checks of
persons hired to become commissioned
peace officers (CPOs) with TABC.
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is authorized
to obtain a criminal history record
information from DPS must refer to its
agency's legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of this
information.

series_title:
Criminal History Checks -
CPOs, Not Hired
series_description:
Pre-employment background checks of
persons not hired to become
commissioned peace officers (CPOs) with
TABC.
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is authorized
to obtain a criminal history record
information from DPS must refer to its
agency's legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of this
information.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, certifications,
licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar
records documenting the training, testing,
certification, licensing, or continuing
education achievements of an employee.

Federal reporting form (Form I-9) for
commissioned peace officers (CPOs).

Federal reporting form (Form I-9) for non-
CPOs.

Summary of apprenticeship applicant
qualifications, evaluation basis for
selection or rejection, original applications,
job assignments, promotions, separations,
layoffs, terminations, compensation
records, conditions of work, time records,
hours of training provided (separate from
labor), minority status, and gender of all
selected and rejected applicants.
Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.

Form completed and signed by employee
or official, or former employee or official,
electing to keep home address, home
telephone number, social security number,
and family information open or
confidential under the Public Information
Act, Government Code, 552.024.
comments:
CAUTION: Most recent public access
election information must be kept as
long as the former employee
verification records. See RSIN 3.3.011.

series_description:
Consultation records, notes, letters,
memos, emails, reports, and other
documentation.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaint filed with EEOC (Equal
Employment Opportunity Commission).
If matter becomes a grievance or the
subject of counseling or litigation, or
employee is subject to disciplinary
action, the records are subject to
retention in the appropriate records
series. See RSIN 1.1.048, 3.1.006,
3.1.018, 3.1.020, or 3.1.021 or AIN
EMP3026a/b/c/d/e or EMP3027.

series_title:
Employee Drug Testing
and Screening Records –
Positive Results and
Calibration

Employee Drug Testing
and Screening Records –
Collection Records
series_description:
Records related to the alcohol and
controlled substances collection process
(except calibration of evidential breath
testing devices).
comments:
See RSIN 3.1.040a for calibration
documentation.

series_title:
Employee Drug Testing
and Screening Records –
Negative Results
series_description:
Records of negative and canceled
controlled substances test results and
alcohol test results with a concentration of
less than 0.02.

Agreements between employee and
agency authorizing certain actions,
including acknowledgement forms,
telecommuting agreements,
outside/secondary employment
authorizations, off-duty assignments
(approved/denied) of commissioned peace
officers (CPOs), or other documentation
that show proof of receipt and awareness
of and adherence to agency policies and
procedures.

Employee or applicant requests for
reasonable accommodation under the ADA
(Americans with Disabilities Act).

Records documenting the initiation,
investigation, and disposition of internal
affairs investigations of alleged misconduct
by non-CPO employees subject to internal
affairs investigations.

Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required deductions
from payroll, including garnishment or
other court-ordered attachments.

Payroll records and registers documenting
employee earnings, wages, and pay. This
may include but is not limited to payroll
input records, summary statements,
payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and
payroll history.

Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.

Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or
in the computation of taxes.

Employer's copy of "Employee’s
Withholding Exemption Certificate."

Records defining the wage or salary rate
for each position in the agency expressed
in dollars, grades, or step numbers.

Records and documentation relating to
unemployment compensation claims,
including reimbursement of funds
disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission
(TWC).

Forms used to deposit employee’s earnings
into a specified personal account.

Records documenting the amount of
pension or deferred compensation earned
by individual employees.

Affirmative action plans for regular
employees.

Affirmative action plans for apprenticeship
programs.

Reports providing statistical information on
labor force.

Minimum information needed to verify
employment. Includes: name; social
security number; exact dates of
employment; last known address; and
most recent public access authorization.
comments: See RSIN 3.1.038.

series_description:
Records relating to review and monitoring
of job classifications within an agency.

Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules,
rosters, or assignments.

Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.

Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed.

Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define
agency wide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of an agency.

Any documents detailing the procedural
duties and responsibilities of agency
positions on a position-by-position basis.

Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listings of all staff by program or name,
staff hired during a month, detailed listings
of employees within its organizational
structure, position vacancies, analyses of
turnover rates and seasonality of
employment, etc.

Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and
checklists required of job applicants or of
current personnel to qualify for promotion
or transfer, including validation records.
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each different
master test (different in terms of either
questions or administration procedures)
should be retained for the period
indicated.

series_description:
Completed aptitude, competency, or skills
test papers and checklists of job applicants
or of current personnel taking a test to
qualify for promotion or transfer.

Includes documentation used to complete
EEO (Equal Employment Opportunity)
reports.

Reports, studies, aggregated or summary
data, and similar documentation compiled
to monitor and demonstrate compliance
with the federal Equal Pay Act.

Diversity and recruitment plans and related
workforce analyses.

Used to create employee leave balances,
to transfer leave balances when an
employee transfers positions, to adjust
carry-over balances at end of fiscal year, to
correct errors on leave accumulation, and
to close out leave accounts on separated
employees.

Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle
and provides employee leave status
information for each position.

Records created to schedule time worked
by employees outside of or in addition to
their regular working hours, including
approval authorizations.
comments:
CAUTION: Only includes overtime
schedules and authorizations. See RSIN
3.4.006 for timekeeping records.

series_description:
Records documenting individual
employee’s hours worked, including work
schedules and documentation evidencing
adherence to or deviation from normal
hours for those employees working on
fixed schedules.

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick Leave
Requests
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for vacation,
compensatory, sick, Family and Medical
Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and
other types of authorized leave, and
supporting documentation.

Records documenting number of hours
transferred in and out of comprehensive
sick leave pool.

Claims; invoices; statements; copies of
checks and purchase orders; expenditure
authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance,
purchase, general journal, expenditure,
and special vouchers; and similar records
that serve to document disbursements,
including those documenting claims for
and reimbursement to employees for
travel and other expenses.

Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants,
cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts
books, cash transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt.

Internal and external periodic fiscal
management reports and associated
worksheets, including federal financial
reports, salary reports, HUB reports,
investment performance reports, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: This series does not include
fiscal reports created to fulfill grant
requirements. See RSIN 4.7.008.

series_description:
Annual Financial Reports (AFR) required by
the General Appropriations Act (100 Day
Report).
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
Annual Financial Reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program per 13
TAC 3.3(a)(2)(a). Working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Periodic reports compiled from
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).

Yearly report compiled from information
entered into the Uniform Statewide
Accounting System (USAS).

Sufficient records to verify information on
unclaimed property previously reported to
the State Comptroller showing the name
and last known address of the apparent
owner of reportable unclaimed property, a
brief description of the property, and the
balance of each unclaimed account, if
appropriate.

Records documenting the internal and
external procedural requirements with
respect to the accounting department of a
state agency.

Records of accounts deemed uncollectible,
including write-off authorizations and
returned checks, warrants, and drafts.

Documentation relating to the capital and
fixed assets of a state agency, including
equipment or property history cards
containing data on initial cost, depreciation
schedules or summaries used for capital
outlay budgeting, and property sale,
auction, or disposal records of agency
owned equipment and property.

Records documenting requests for
payment of a sum of money according to
the terms of a policy or contract.

This series documents state, federal, and
other sponsored grant projects
participated in or administered by state
agencies. It includes grant authorization
records, which provide evidence of the
award of grants to or by agencies;
grant/project financial or performance
reports, which are periodic reports of
financial activity and/or program
performance related to grants received or
made by agencies.
comments:
CAUTION: Retention requirements may
vary depending on the specific funding
agency. Records in this series may be
subject to Government Code, 441.1855.
Agencies must ensure that records are
retained for the appropriate retention
period.

series_description:
Signature cards or similar records
establishing authority of an agency
employee to initiate or authorize financial
transactions on behalf of an agency.

Logs documenting requests and receipt of
FTI, including any information created by
the recipient that is derived from federal
return or return information received from
the IRS or obtained through a secondary
source.

Indirect cost plan and supporting
documentation created or maintained in
the development of the plan.

Bank statements, credit card statements,
cancelled checks, check registers, deposit
slips, debit and credit notices,
reconciliations, and other banking related
records.

Required by the General Appropriations
Act. Includes encumbrances and
documentation about budget transfers and
revisions, as well as detail charts of
accounts.

Includes laboratory reports, submission
records, disposition and destruction
records.
comments:
CAUTION: If a laboratory record
becomes the subject of an
administrative action, litigation,
Enforcement or Compliance
investigation, or label approval/product
registration process it must be included
in and is subject to the minimum
retention period of RSIN 1.1.048, RSIN
1.1.073, AIN ADM1005a/b, AIN
ADM1006a/b/c, or AIN ADM1013.

series_description:
Includes product samples submitted to
TABC as part of the label approval/product
registration process. Also includes samples
collected during Enforcement or
Compliance actions.

Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction except for buildings.
Documents include specifications,
affidavits of publication of calls for bids,
performance bonds, contracts, purchase
orders, inspection reports, and
correspondence. May also include other
applicable documentation in the master
contract file per Texas Comptroller of
Public Accounts Contract Management
Guide.
comments:
See related RSIN 5.3.007a/c for bid
documentation. See RSIN 5.1.017 for
contract logs.

series_description:
Records documenting incoming or
outgoing deliveries, including through
private courier services.

Any mailing address, telephone or fax
number, or email address records
maintained by an agency on its employees
or on entities or persons it serves.

Records and reports of postage expenses,
including postal meter usage.

Licenses and permits obtained from
external agencies or organizations in order
to perform operations.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include licenses and
permits issued by an agency as part of
its statutory responsibilities.

series_description:
Schedules of prices charged by an agency
for services to the public or other agencies,
including any documentation used to
determine the charges.

Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile,
and other policies for government property
and personnel including supporting
documentation relevant to the
implementation, modification, renewal, or
replacement of policies.

Any internally distributed manual,
guidelines, or similar records that establish
standard office procedures for an agency,
e.g. agency style manuals, telephone
protocols, mail room procedures, print
shop and photocopy ordering instructions.

Any record created by an agency to track
any type of incoming and outgoing
correspondence or packages by the U.S.
Postal Service (USPS) or by private
couriers.

List of agency contracts, leases, and
agreements, including general obligation,
land lease, utilities, and construction
contracts.

Surveillance videos of buildings, facilities,
vehicles, or other state property.
comments:
See RSIN 5.4.001 or 5.4.014a/b if video
is needed for an accident investigation
or RSIN 1.1.048 if the video is needed as
evidence in litigation.
The disposal of surveillance videos need
not be documented through
destruction signoffs (RSIN 1.2.001) or in
records disposition logs (RSIN 1.2.010).

series_description:
Includes architectural and engineering
drawings, profiles, and blueprints of
planning, design, construction, conversion,
or modernization of leased facilities,
structures, infrastructure, and systems.

Records documenting the determination,
checking, or rectifying of any instrument
giving quantitative measurements.
comments:
CAUTION: Some equipment and
instruments may require longer
retention period. State agencies must
determine if longer retention period is
required based on the type of
equipment or instruments they use
within their agency.

series_description:
Records documenting the inventorying,
maintenance, usage, and disposal of
supplies, equipment, and property of a
state agency. Includes lost, stolen, and
damage reports.
comments:
See RSIN 5.2.008 for the maintenance
logs of individual pieces of equipment.

series_title:
Inspection, Repair, and
Maintenance Records –
Equipment
series_description:
Equipment history file, which may include
logbooks and requests for installation,
moves, service, repair, etc.
comments:
For service agreements or contracts
related to equipment repairs and
service, retain in accordance with RSIN
5.1.001 For vehicle maintenance
records, see RSIN 5.6.003.

series_title:
Estimate Files (Supply and
Repair Cost Estimates)
series_description:
Quotes for minor construction and repair
projects and supplies. Includes supporting
documentation, as applicable.

Listing shows all additions, changes,
deletions, and transfer times for the
monthly processing period.
comments:
See RSIN 5.2.006 for annual inventory
listing.

series_description:
Requests or work orders for repairs or
maintenance to facilities, vehicles, or
equipment completed by internal agency
personnel or external service providers.

Any type of usage report or log used to
monitor utilities such as gas, electric,
water, etc.
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include utility usage
reports for agencies that operate their
own utilities.

series_description:
Equipment and material descriptions and
specifications that may include but are not
limited to detailed descriptions; lists of raw
materials and ingredients; physical
characteristics of items; special handling
procedures; or technical drawings.

Documentation of mail returned by the
postal or other mail delivery services as
undeliverable.
comments:
CAUTION: If corrected address provided
and mail rerouted, maintain address
update documentation for prescribed
retention period.

series_description:
Freight or cargo claims against carrier for
damage or loss to a shipment.

Shipping information, including order
acknowledgements, packing slips, and
related documentation.
comments:
CAUTION: Some shipping information
may require longer retention period.
State agencies must determine if longer
retention period is required based on
the type of materials shipped to or from
their agency.

series_description:
Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, awarded and unawarded
bids, and bid tabulation /evaluations.

Invalid bids that do not meet agency
submission requirements and are not
included in bid evaluation process (e.g.
withdrawn, missed submission deadline,
incomplete submission, etc.)

Log, register, etc., providing a record of
purchase orders issued, orders received,
and similar data on procurement status.

Requests for information preliminary to
the procurement of goods or services by
direct purchase or bid.
comments:
See RSIN 1.1.020 and 1.1.021 for public
information requests.
CAUTION: If the request for information
leads to request for proposal or bid, the
request for information documentation
should be retained in accordance with
RSIN 5.3.007a/c.

series_description:
W-9 IRS Form used to request a taxpayer
identification number (TIN) for reporting
information to the Internal Revenue
Service (IRS). This includes W-9 forms
received by a state agency from vendors.

series_title:
Occupational Accident
Reports and Associated
Documentation
series_description:
Accident or occupational disease reports
(by supervisors and employees) and other
associated reports required to be
submitted to the State Office of Risk
Management (SORM) or its predecessors
or maintained internally on accident
frequency.
comments:
See RSIN 5.4.014a/b for non-employee
accidents.

series_description:
Plans for evacuation of agency facilities in
cases of emergency.

Fire, safety, and other inspection records
of agency facilities and equipment,
including orders issued by inspectors to
correct deficiencies in compliance with any
code or regulations.

Records of training given to employees in
an agency hazard communications
program. May include but is not limited to
date of class, roster of attendees, subjects
covered, and instructors.
comments:
See RSIN 3.1.027 for individual
employee training records.

series_description:
Plan that provides information to
employees about the hazardous chemicals
to which they may be exposed to in their
workplace.

Logs, registers, or similar records
documenting visitors to limited access or
restricted areas of agency facilities.

Records relating to the issuance of keys,
identification cards, building passes,
passwords, signed statements or similar
instruments of access to agency or state
facilities, equipment or automated
systems.

Plans developed (per Labor Code, 412.054)
to mitigate the damage of potential events
that could endanger an agency’s ability to
function. Includes disaster preparedness,
response, and recovery plans.
comments:
See RSIN 5.4.017 for records related to
responding or recovering from an
emergency.

series_description:
Reports of accidents to adults on state
property or in any other situation in which
the state agency could be a party to a
lawsuit.

Reports of accidents to minors on state
property or in any other situation in which
the state agency could be a party to a
lawsuit.

This series documents the events and
damages to institutional property due to
storms, riots, fires, droughts, floods, and
other events affecting citizens and facilities
within the jurisdiction of the institution.
This series may include but is not limited
to: diaries; logs; reports; photographs;
notes which indicate or document what
happened, when, and where; and related
documentation and correspondence.
comments:
See RSIN 5.4.013 for Continuity of
Operations Plans (COOP).
CAUTION: If grant monies are received
to assist with the response or recovery,
the retention requirements for this
series may vary depending on the
specific funding agency.

series_description:
Includes working papers and agency risk
assessment used to develop the plan, per
Texas Internal Auditing Act requirement.
comments:
See RSIN 1.1.002 for individual Audit
records not related to the development
of the Audit Plan.

series_description:
Documents collected or generated as part
of the process of reviewing other state
agency internal audit programs. Includes
but is not limited to: self-assessments,
worksheets, surveys or questionnaires,
evaluations, and other documents as
described in the State Agency Internal
Audit Forum (SAIAF) Peer Review Manual.
comments:
See RSIN 1.1.002 for individual Audit
records not related to the peer review
of an Audit Plan.

series_title:
Inspection, Repair, and
Maintenance Records –
Vehicles
series_description:
Records and documentation related to
inspections, repairs, and maintenance for
state vehicles.
comments:
See RSIN 5.2.008 for non-vehicle
equipment maintenance records.

series_description: Includes authorized driver list.

Includes mileage, fuel/oil consumption,
passengers carried and other related
operational information.

Vehicle titles, registration information, and
owner manuals for state vehicles.

Records documenting issuance of parking
permits and assignments.
